mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate income expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns (Rank IC consistently near zero across rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.3% force expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.3% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals underlying demand resilience, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS spread fails to predict 20-day return direction with accuracy above 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising unemployment indicates a supply-side contraction that immediately depresses equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO and positive UNRATE changes do not predict negative 5-day returns during NBER-defined recession onset periods.
